content.full_text:
Montereyans for Affordable Housing
P.O. Box 2776
Monterey, CA 93942
375-6898

January 30, 1991

Blanca Breeze
Legal Division
Fair Political
Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95084

Dear Blanca,

As per our phone conversation today I am requesting a written
confirmation of the information you provided me.

As we discussed I would like to do the following with my committee (ID# 903803).

1. Terminate the committee, disposing our current funds
in compliance with state laws.

2. Retain the same name and bank account.

3. Enter into a lawsuit as the plaintiff challenging a
ballot measure that was passed by the voters in the
November, 1990 election.

4. Use the committee's bank account to raise funds in
order to pay for the cost of the suit.

It is my understanding that this would be in compliance with
State law, and since the committee is challenging a current law,
not a ballot measure, that no reporting of the funds raised is
required.

Thank you for all your help in this matter. I look forward to
your reply.

Sincerely,

Luke Fry
Chairperson

Retain the same name and bank account. 3. Enter into a lawsuit as the plaintiff challenging a ballot measure that was passed by the voters in the November, 1990 election. 4. Use the committee's bank account to raise funds in order to pay for the cost of the suit. It is my understanding that this would be in compliance with state law, and since the committee is challenging a current law, not a ballot measure, that no reporting of the funds raised is required.
sections.question_synthetic: A committee seeks to terminate while retaining its name and bank account, then use that account to raise funds for a lawsuit challenging a ballot measure approved in November 1990. The requester believes this would comply with state law without requiring fund reporting.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from a committee seeking legal guidance, not an FPPC response. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections are present. The document appears to be a follow-up to a prior phone conversation with FPPC staff member Blanca Breeze. No FPPC response is included in this document.

embedding.summary: Luke F., chairperson of Montereyans for Affordable Housing committee (ID# 903803), requests written confirmation from FPPC Legal Division regarding plans to terminate the committee while retaining its assets to fund a lawsuit challenging a November 1990 ballot measure.
